title: atractocarpus baladicus
intro: Atractocarpus baladicus, also known as the 'Gingerbread Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Atractocarpus baladicus include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to support the immune system. Atractocarpus baladicus can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions or decoctions, and has been used in traditional Australian medicine to treat various ailments.
